key_insight: Laser welding depth strongly affects lap-joint strength, so a calibrated FEM model can predict joint quality from process parameters.
engineering_relevance: The study supports process design for laser-welded stainless-steel lap joints by linking weld penetration, measured strength, and simulation-based prediction.
method: Built lap-joint specimens from X5CrNi18-10 steel sheets (100×200×2 mm).
Performed laser welding at two penetration levels: full and partial penetration.
Measured mechanical strength and examined weld macrostucture, then created Abaqus FEM discrete models and validated them against experiments.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experimental strength tests and macroscopic examinations on full- and partial-penetration joints, followed by numerical simulation results compared directly with experimental data for validation.
watch_item: The model is validated only on two welding-depth cases for a specific stainless-steel lap joint, so broader applicability to other geometries, materials, or parameter ranges needs further confirmation.
paper_title: Analysis of the Influence of Laser Welding Parameters on the Quality of the Lap Joint
